title: RabbitMQ Policy and Operator Policy Change Events
dataSources: RabbitMQ node log lines referencing policy application (`sourcetype=rabbitmq:log`)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder file monitor on RabbitMQ nodes
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="rabbitmq:log"
| search "policy" AND ("set" OR "applied" OR "updated" OR "cleared" OR "operator policy")
| table _time, host, _raw
description: Policy changes alter TTL, queue type, delivery limits, and federation behavior—high-impact operational events that should align with change management.
value: Creates an immutable Splunk-backed audit trail when queue semantics shift without a matching ticket.
implementation: Validate log verbosity includes policy changes in your RabbitMQ version. Deduplicate noisy HA events. Join against CMDB ownership for routing.
visualization: Timeline (policy events), Table (recent changes), Single value (events per week).
grandmaExplanation: We record when global rules on queues change so teams can explain sudden message behavior.
